tokenEstimate.method: Sample TSX files, run `fooks extract --model-payload`, scale sampled bytes to repo-level proxy.

environmentParity.runner: Selected with --runner; vanilla and fooks variants use the same runner.
environmentParity.prompt: Identical task text for vanilla and fooks variants.
environmentParity.codexHome: Each variant uses an isolated CODEX_HOME with auth.json and config.toml symlinked from the same host account.
environmentParity.variantDifference: The fooks variant runs fooks init/scan/attach before the same agent command; vanilla does not.

scale.critical: Do not cite without fixing measurement.

risks.name: sample_size
risks.interpretation: Use as round-1 proof only; repeat N>=3 before claiming stable performance.

risks.name: environment_parity
risks.interpretation: Both variants use the same selected runner with isolated CODEX_HOME; fooks additionally prepares native fooks context.

risks.name: orchestration_overhead
risks.interpretation: Direct Codex is the cleaner product benchmark; OMX runner includes orchestration overhead and policy surface.

risks.name: cleanup_reproducibility
risks.interpretation: Worktree residue can contaminate reruns if cleanup fails.

risks.name: wall_clock_noise
risks.evidence: Single-run wall-clock timing includes model/service variability.
risks.interpretation: Prefer median and p95 over repeated identical tasks.

risks.name: runtime_token_claim
risks.evidence: Actual OMX/Codex tokens available; fooks used fewer runtime tokens in 1/1 pair (29% less: 63435 -> 44970).
risks.interpretation: Runtime token reduction observed, but N=1 sample. Repeat N>=3 before claiming stable runtime token savings. Keep proxy compression and actual tokens separate in reporting.

tokens.method: Sample TSX files, run `fooks extract --model-payload`, scale sampled bytes to repo-level proxy.

Changes made:
- Changed the confirmation input to `type="email"`.
- Added inline red validation text after the user starts typing:
  - malformed email: existing `auth.verification-requested.invalid_email_address`
  - non-matching email: existing `environments.actions.does_not_exactly_match`
- Added invalid input styling and ARIA wiring.
- Kept deletion disabled while the email is invalid or deletion is already running.
- Added a guard in `deleteAccount()` so form submission cannot bypass the disabled state.

- `pnpm exec eslint ...` and `pnpm exec prettier ...` could not run because those binaries are not installed in this checkout (`Command "eslint" not found`, `Command "prettier" not found`).

fooks.stdout_tail:
Implemented the account deletion confirmation validation in [index.tsx](/Users/veluga/Workspace/fooks-test-repos/.worktrees/bm-T5-fooks-1776311592/apps/web/modules/account/components/DeleteAccountModal/index.tsx).

Changes made:
- Switched the confirmation input to `type="email"`.
- Added inline red validation text under the input after the user types a malformed or non-matching email.
- Reused existing translation keys.
- Kept deletion disabled when the email is invalid or deletion is already running.
- Added a guard so `deleteAccount()` returns early if invoked while invalid/disabled.

Verification:
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Targeted `prettier`/`eslint` commands could not run because those binaries are not available in this checkout via `pnpm exec`.
